document_info.title: U14 Boys Costa Rica Tour - Training Session 2

session_practices.name: Technical Warm-Up: High-Tempo Combination, Turn & Move

pitch_diagram.image_description: A square 25x25 meter training grid. Four corner cones are labeled A, B, C, and D. In the exact center of the grid, a smaller 4x4 meter box is marked out using tall yellow cones to simulate a heavily congested central midfield zone.

tactical_layout.dimensions: 25m x 25m outer grid, 4m x 4m central zone
tactical_layout.equipment: 4 tall yellow cones forming the inner constraint
tactical_layout.stations: 4 corner positions executing continuous diagonal passing patterns

setup.player_distribution: Players are distributed evenly among the four outer corner stations. Two balls start at opposite corners (A and C) to maximize technical volume and force players to scan before receiving.

session_practices.explanation: Player A plays a firm, low pass diagonally into the central box for Player B, who has checked away from their corner. Player B must receive the ball inside the yellow cone box, execute a quick turn (e.g., inside hook, outside flick, or Cruyff turn), and open up their body to play a pass out to Player C. Players follow their pass to rotate to the next station.
coaching_points: Passes into the central box must be hit with pace to mimic breaking an opponent's midfield line.
The receiving player must check their shoulder to scan behind them before entering the central box.
The first touch inside the box must be soft and directional, turning the ball smoothly into the path of the next passing target.

session_practices.name: 3v1 to 3v2 Rondo: Overload and Penetrate

pitch_diagram.image_description: A rectangular 30x15 meter grid divided cleanly into two equal 15x15 meter halves by a dashed center line. Two small mini-gates made of cones sit on each deep baseline. A 3v1 overload is established in the initial possession grid.

setup.player_roles: 3 attacking players (blue) and 1 defending player (orange) occupy the active half. 1 resting defender stands inside the opposite half ready to engage when the ball crosses the line.

session_practices.explanation: The 3 blue attackers circulate the ball inside their half, attempting to keep possession against the 1 orange defender. After completing a sequence of 4 passes, they look for an opening to pass through the mini-gates or dribble across the center line. As soon as the ball enters the opposite half, the resting defender becomes active, and 1 supporting attacker joins to create a live 3v2 matching block. If defenders win the ball, they score by passing back across the center line.
coaching_points: Use quick one-touch and two-touch ball speed to stretch the lone defender and open up a forward passing lane.
The ball carrier must show patience, committing the defender before executing a line-breaking pass.
Upon crossing the center line, attackers must split wide quickly to establish maximum width and exploit the 3v2 overload.

session_practices.name: Phase of Play: Creative Central Combinations (5v4)

pitch_diagram.image_description: The layout spans two-thirds of a standard field. Play is restricted away from the extreme flanks using vertical cone lines to create a wide central corridor. A full goal with a goalkeeper is positioned at the baseline, protected by a back 4 low block (4 defenders). The attacking unit fields a midfield trio and a front pair (5 attackers).

setup.player_distribution: 5 attackers line up in a 3-2 shape facing a compact 4-man defensive backline. Play triggers from a deep central playmaker near the halfway line.

session_practices.explanation: The attacking midfield trio uses quick lateral ball circulation to shift the 4-man defensive block from side to side. The primary objective is to find a window to slip a penetrative pass into the feet of the two strikers. The strikers must work in tandem—one dropping deep into the pocket to link play, while the other makes an explosive blind-side run behind the center-backs to receive and finish.
coaching_points: Strikers must coordinate their movements on different vertical lines to disrupt the central defenders' markers.
Midfielders must use disguised passes (looking wide, passing central) to unlock tight vertical gaps in the defensive line.
The receiving striker must adopt a half-turned body shape, allowing them to spin and strike or lay off on one touch.

session_practices.name: 11v11 Match with Zone 14 Scoring Incentives

pitch_diagram.image_description: A full-field competitive 11v11 match. An area measuring 20x15 meters directly outside the opponent's penalty box—traditionally known as 'Zone 14'—is highlighted with dashed lines to govern specific tactical scoring rewards.

setup.initial_alignment: Both squads line up in their standard competitive systems (e.g., matching 4-3-3 or 4-2-3-1 frameworks), operating under high game intensity and standard rules.

session_practices.explanation: A regular match with a tactical twist: any normal goal counts as 1 point. However, if a team successfully plays a pass into an attacking player inside the designated 'Zone 14' box, and that player combines to score within 3 touches, the goal counts for 3 points. This constraint encourages fast, central combination play in high-value creative zones.
coaching_points: Midfielders must look forward early, prioritizing vertical or diagonal entry passes into Zone 14 over safe possession.
Attacking players inside Zone 14 must maintain extreme technical composure, using one-touch combinations or quick turns to unlock the box.
Defensively, the backline and midfield must compress the vertical gap between them to completely deny the opponent space inside Zone 14.
